result:
I would make the truth matter more than power.

Not “truth” as whatever side you’re on—just the plain, stubborn, verifiable kind. If one world-wide change could ripple furthest, I’d wire human systems (markets, governments, media, even our own memories) so that distortion for gain simply rebounds. The bigger the lie, the harder the recoil. No hidden fortunes built on concealed harms, no demagogues protected by fog, no ad campaign that can sell self-destruction as freedom.

Everything else—climate cooperation, livable wages, cures for diseases we ignore because they’re profitable—depends on that single pivot: letting reality speak louder than those who can afford to muffle it.

The bigger the lie, the harder the recoil. No hidden fortunes built on concealed harms, no demagogues protected by fog, no ad campaign that can sell self-destruction as freedom.

Everything else—climate cooperation, livable wages, cures for diseases we ignore because they’re profitable—depends on that single pivot: letting reality speak louder than those who can afford to muffle it.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
